backlogItem: Legacy threshold hotfix bundle (US-140-144 via US-220) + US-211 integration (US-221) + TD-030 close (US-222) + TD-031 dead-code deletion (US-223) + record_drain_test CLI default (US-224) + TD-034 US-216 deeper stage wiring (US-225). Spool parallel deliverables tracked in sprintNotes.

testBaseline.note: Post-Sprint-16 close baseline: Ralph's Session 90 handoff reports fast suite 3079 (+72 over Sprint 15 baseline 3007, 0 regressions). Sprint 16 merged to main@de9379d on 2026-04-22 plus deploy-script fixes (a0d6c30 server migration local-run + ea6e71e Pi systemd unit install-if-changed).

sprintNotes: GOAL: close aged safety debt, complete Sprint 16's US-211 integration, trim dead code Spool's power audit surfaced. 3 of Spool's top-priority tuning-domain asks land this sprint: safety-threshold hotfix bundle, BT resilience actually wired, dead BatteryMonitor gone. 3 smaller tidy-ups ride with: config key-path fix, CLI default flip, US-216 stage behaviors proper-wired.

EXECUTION ORDER (all stories independent; Ralph picks any): US-220 (legacy thresholds, M P0) + US-221 (US-211 integration, S P0) anchor; US-225 (TD-034 stage wiring, M P1) is heaviest cleanup; US-222 + US-223 + US-224 (S P1 each) fit around. No cross-story dependencies.

THEME: post-Sprint 16 the Pi runs real in production but two known gaps remain: US-211's in-process BT recovery is unwired (every flap still bounces via Restart=always) and the Sprint-3 legacy thresholds still carry unsafe values that any future consult would trigger. Sprint 17 closes both. Additionally Spool's Session 6/7 audits surfaced 3 TDs (030, 031, 034) which land alongside. Sprint is mostly small-cap work -- no L stories this sprint, no Spool-audit-gates.

CONSTRAINTS: US-220 values come STRAIGHT from Spool's variance report (Session 3 2026-04-12). Ralph does NOT re-derive or second-guess them; if any value looks off, STOP and file inbox. US-221 preserves US-211's classifier + backoff exactly as shipped (Spool's minor Sprint 16 spec-drift flag accepted). US-223 deletes only dead code confirmed by Spool's audit -- NOT LIVE UpsMonitor / ShutdownHandler / BatteryHealthRecorder / PowerDownOrchestrator.

GROUNDING SOURCES: Spool's Sprint 17 tuning-priorities note (primary), Sprint 16 release-readiness note (US-211 YELLOW detail), Session 3 code-audit-variances (US-140-144 values), TDs 030/031/034. Ralph Agent 2's US-216 completion note details the TD-034 wiring gaps.

SPOOL PARALLEL DELIVERABLES (tracked here, NOT Ralph stories): (a) First real-drive review ritual execution once CIO drill produces captured data -- Spool runs US-219 ritual against real drive_id + grades Ollama output against DESIGN_NOTE.md 6 quality gates + delivers findings + may propose prompt iteration. (b) DSM DTC interpretation cheat sheet -- now unblocked by Sprint 15 US-204; Spool post-drill docs work. (c) TD-033 telemetry_logger <-> UpsMonitor audit -- 20-min Spool deliverable. All three are Spool lanes; PM tracks completion via inbox.

POST-SPRINT-17 PAYOFF: legacy threshold safety debt cleared (no dormant hazards), US-211 resilience actually protects drives (not aspirational), dead BatteryMonitor + battery_log gone (~700 LOC + false-confidence hazard), Pi auto-shutdown ladder has proper graceful-degradation behaviors wired. First real drive after Sprint 17 exercises the FULL pipeline including in-process BT resilience AND staged shutdown with drive-protection behaviors.

stories.title: Legacy threshold hotfix bundle -- US-140 + US-141 + US-142 + US-143 + US-144 safety fixes
stories.intent: Ship the 5 safety-critical threshold hotfixes Spool identified in her Session 3 code-audit (2026-04-12) that have aged 10 days without landing. None are load-bearing today because the legacy profile threshold system isn't consulted by live code, but all are dormant-but-dangerous: any future use triggers unsafe behavior. Bundle resolves US-140 (coolantTempCritical: 110 nonsense), US-141 (rpmRedline 7200 -> 7000), US-142 (boostPressureMax 18psi -> 15psi), US-143 (display boost stubs BOOST_CAUTION=18.0/BOOST_DANGER=22.0 -> 14/15), US-144 (INJECTOR_CAUTION 80% -> 75%). Per Spool's recommendation, one bundled story beats five scattered micro-stories.

src/pi/profile/manager.py or wherever profile defaults seed (coolantTempCritical across 6 files per Spool audit -- rg finds the sites)
src/pi/display/primary_screen.py or display stubs (BOOST_CAUTION + BOOST_DANGER constants: 18.0/22.0 -> 14.0/15.0 psi)

stories.title: US-211 integration wiring -- handleCaptureError() into data_logger.py capture loop
stories.intent: US-211 (Sprint 16) shipped BtResilienceMixin.handleCaptureError + classifier (ADAPTER_UNREACHABLE / ECU_SILENT / FATAL) + reconnect loop with backoff schedule + canonical connection_log event types. Ralph flagged in his completion note that the classifier is NOT wired into the actual capture loop at data_logger.py. Today every BT flap still triggers US-210's Restart=always systemd bounce (process death + restart) rather than US-211's in-process recovery (same PID, retry). Until this wiring lands, US-211's value is aspirational. Spool YELLOW-graded US-211 in the Sprint 16 release review specifically on this gap. Must ship before any new live drill expects in-process BT resilience.

src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/core.py runLoop (if the poll-tier dispatch lives here rather than logger: same classifier integration)

Mode 01 PID polling tiers (US-199 scheduler stays; ECU_SILENT backoff is a poll-cadence modifier, not a tier reshape)

groundingRefs.value: US-211 classifier buckets: ADAPTER_UNREACHABLE / ECU_SILENT / FATAL
Process MUST NOT exit on ADAPTER_UNREACHABLE or ECU_SILENT; only FATAL surfaces to systemd
Same PID across BT-flap gap (integration test asserts)
connection_log timeline: bt_disconnect -> adapter_wait -> reconnect_attempt -> reconnect_success on successful recovery

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg --files-with-matches 'handleCaptureError|BtResilienceMixin|ADAPTER_UNREACHABLE' src/ tests/ to map current integration state. Record filesActuallyTouched.
Capture loop at data_logger.py (or orchestrator runLoop if that's the true capture site) catches OBD exceptions at the poll boundary and routes through US-211 classifier + handleCaptureError.
ADAPTER_UNREACHABLE path: log bt_disconnect to connection_log, invoke reconnect_loop.waitForAdapter, resume poll on success -- WITHOUT process exit.

ECU_SILENT path: log ecu_silent_wait to connection_log, keep connection, back off poll cadence (reduce poll rate; Ralph picks conservative multiplier per Spool's spec), resume normal cadence on ECU response.
FATAL path: log + re-raise; systemd Restart=always (US-210) handles the bounce.
Integration test test_bt_flap_in_process.py: inject fake BT drop; assert SAME PID before / during / after; assert connection_log shows canonical event sequence.
Unit test test_capture_loop_integration.py: exhaustive exception-class routing coverage (one test per bucket + edge cases like rfcomm timeout, disconnected OBD(), python-obd NO_DATA).
specs/architecture.md Collector Resilience section updated with integration diagram + example log timeline.
docs/testing.md BT drop-resilience walkthrough shows in-process recovery, not systemd bounce, as the expected operator observation.
Fast suite + new tests pass, 0 regressions; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors.

stories.title: TD-030 close -- pi.hardware.enabled config key path one-liner fix
stories.intent: Close TD-030 (filed Spool Session 6 audit). lifecycle.py:450 reads self._config.get('hardware', {}).get('enabled', True) — top-level 'hardware' key. But config.json puts hardware under pi.hardware. Works today only by accident: the missing top-level key returns default True. Any future attempt to disable hardware via config silently fails. One-line fix: change to self._config.get('pi', {}).get('hardware', {}).get('enabled', True). Add a regression test pinning the new behavior on both enabled=true and enabled=false paths.

Hardware initialization logic itself
config.json structure

pi.hardware.enabled is the canonical config path (mirrors other pi.* subsections)

stories.title: TD-031 close -- delete dead BatteryMonitor + src/pi/power/battery.py + battery_log table
stories.intent: Close TD-031 by deleting dead code Spool's power audit surfaced. BatteryMonitor (690 LOC in src/pi/power/battery.py) is never instantiated in production (pi.batteryMonitoring.enabled=false in config). Its voltage thresholds (11.0V/11.5V) are 12V automotive or 3S Li values -- this hardware is a 1S LiPo on MAX17048 operating 3.0-4.3V. If an operator ever flips enabled=true expecting battery protection, they get zero coverage. US-216's Power-Down Orchestrator now fully supersedes this class for the shutdown-ladder domain, so the dead code can be removed without functional loss. Deleting reduces audit surface + closes a false-confidence hazard.

stories.title: record_drain_test.py CLI default flip -- --load-class production -> test
stories.intent: Manual invocation of scripts/record_drain_test.py is almost always a CIO-driven scheduled drill (load_class='test'), not a real power-loss event (load_class='production'). The script currently defaults to production, so a CIO who forgets to pass --load-class test during a monthly drill pollutes the production baseline with simulated data. Flip default to test; require explicit --load-class production for the rare case where a human manually records a real drain. One-line change; test update; prevents baseline drift.

doNotTouch: load_class CHECK constraint enum (still ('production','test','sim')) -- only default flips
US-216 orchestrator writer path (stays production for real shutdowns)

stories.title: TD-034 close -- US-216 deeper stage behavior wiring (forceKeyOff + no_new_drives flag + sync force-push)
stories.intent: Close TD-034 (filed by Ralph Agent 2 at US-216 ship). US-216's PowerDownOrchestrator correctly fires the 30/25/20 SOC stages + legacy suppression + the ladder-vs-legacy regression test, but the four downstream stage behaviors are log-only / best-effort today: WARNING's no_new_drives DB flag doesn't exist; WARNING's SyncClient.forcePush() API doesn't exist; IMMINENT's DriveDetector.forceKeyOff() API doesn't exist (EngineStateMachine has it, DriveDetector delegates); IMMINENT's poll-tier pause hook isn't wired. None of these are bug-fix-critical because the TRIGGER -> systemctl poweroff cascade closes everything via normal service-stop paths, but they're graceful-degradation niceties that give the Pi ~30-45s of additional safety margin during drain events. Spool flagged these as real stage-behavior gaps worth closing.

src/pi/data/database_schema.py (new pi_state single-row key-value table OR new column no_new_drives on existing table; Ralph picks pattern per codebase convention)

src/pi/obdii/drive/detector.py (drive-id-mint gate: reads no_new_drives flag; when true, new cranking transitions do NOT mint a new drive_id; engine_state machine still transitions but stays id-less)

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg for current forceKeyOff + SyncClient.push + no_new_drives + pausePolling references; record call sites + integration points.
DriveDetector.forceKeyOff(reason) exists + wraps EngineStateMachine.forceKeyOff + emits drive-end row with reason in connection_log.
no_new_drives flag lives on pi_state table (or equivalent); migration idempotent; get/set helpers + DriveDetector cranking-transition check implemented.
SyncClient.forcePush() API exists + flushes pending deltas + returns summary + respects existing AUTH + timeout configuration.
Orchestrator runLoop pausePolling/resumePolling APIs exist + tier dispatch honors; resumePolling on AC-restore does NOT drop in-flight queries.

PowerDownOrchestrator stage callbacks wire: WARNING -> {set no_new_drives, forcePush}; IMMINENT -> {pausePolling, forceKeyOff, BT-close via US-211}; AC-restore -> {clear no_new_drives, resumePolling, BT reconnect via US-211 upon adapter-reachable}.
Integration test: mocked drain 35->15; each stage callback verifiably calls its new API; AC-restore fully unwinds all stage effects.
Unit tests per new API (forceKeyOff, no_new_drives flag, forcePush, pause/resume polling).
TD-034 annotated Closed.
specs/architecture.md Section 11 updated with stage-behavior wiring details.
Fast suite + new tests pass, 0 regressions; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors.
verification: ruff check src/pi/drive/ src/pi/sync/ src/pi/data/ src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/ src/pi/power/
invariants: AC-restore MUST unwind all stage effects: clear no_new_drives, resume polling, leave collector operational.
no_new_drives does NOT affect currently-active drives -- only gates NEW drive_id minting on cranking transitions.
pausePolling does NOT kill the connection -- poll threads idle but stay attached; resumePolling wakes them.
forcePush is a superset of normal sync, NOT a bypass of correctness -- all normal delta-sync invariants apply.
DriveDetector.forceKeyOff writes a proper drive-end row with a reason field -- downstream analytics see a first-class drive termination, not a gap.
TRIGGER -> poweroff path stays unchanged (US-216 invariant). This story is about WARNING + IMMINENT + AC-restore graceful-degradation.
stopConditions: STOP if adding pausePolling requires significant refactor of the poll-tier dispatcher (thread-unsafe, tightly coupled) -- file inbox note with scope cost; US-225 may split.

STOP if forceKeyOff on DriveDetector conflicts with the existing drive-end detection (30s KEY_OFF debounce from US-200) -- the force path should BYPASS debounce with a reason code, not fight the machine.
STOP if no_new_drives flag persistence requires a new Alembic migration (not just ensure*Table) -- leverage US-213 gate path; do not fork.
STOP if SyncClient.forcePush exposes a state the caller can corrupt (e.g., concurrent-call race) -- add a lock or document the single-writer assumption.
